response:
```python
# test_gcd.py
import pytest

from your_module import gcd  # replace "your_module" with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("a", "b", "expected"),
    [
        (48, 18, 6),
        (18, 48, 6),
        (54, 24, 6),
        (17, 13, 1),
        (13, 13, 13),
        (1, 999, 1),
        (999, 1, 1),
    ],
)
def test_gcd_happy_path(a, b, expected):
    assert gcd(a, b) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("a", "b", "expected"),
    [
        (0, 5, 5),
        (5, 0, 5),
        (0, -5, 5),
        (-5, 0, 5),
        (0, 0, 0),
    ],
)
def test_gcd_with_zero(a, b, expected):
    assert gcd(a, b) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("a", "b", "expected"),
    [
        (-48, 18, 6),
        (48, -18, 6),
        (-48, -18, 6),
        (-7, -3, 1),
        (-12, -12, 12),
    ],
)
def test_gcd_with_negative_numbers(a, b, expected):
    assert gcd(a, b) == expected


def test_gcd_large_numbers():
    assert gcd(1234567890, 9876543210) == 90


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("a", "b"),
    [
        (10, "5"),
        ("10", 5),
        (None, 5),
        (10, None),
        ([10], 5),
        (10, [5]),
    ],
)
def test_gcd_invalid_types_raise_type_error(a, b):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        gcd(a, b)
```
